# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

Resources:
    MySecurityGroup:
        Type: 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup'
        Properties:
            GroupDescription: Allow SSH, HTTP & HTTPS Access from Anywhere 
            
            VpcId: YourVPCID # Replace with your own ID or reference to a subnet that is associated with an existing vpc. This security group will be used by all EC2 instances created within the same region as this one and it's attached only if you want public access via browser outside of AWS
            
            SecurityGroupIngress:  # List for each ingress defined in properties below must have a value associated with them, at least one rule is required. If your configuration requires an outbound allow-all traversal from another region then also add Ingress rules to deny all traffic (0.0.0.0/0)
                - 
                    CidrIp: "24567891" # Replace with actual cidr value which is publicly accessible via browser outside of AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example IP ranges like '0.0.0.0/0' or a specific CIDR (ex: 192.0.X.Y)
                    FromPort: 80 # Replace with the port you want publicly accessible via browser outside of AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example '3456' or any specific outbound connection like (from other ports). Default is all incoming traffic allowed.
                    ToPort: 80 # Replace with the port you want publicly accessible via browser outside of AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example '3456' or any specific outbound connection like (from other ports). Default is all incoming traffic allowed.
                - 
                    CidrIp: "987" # Replace with actual cidr value which are accessible only via SSH from AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example IP ranges like '0.0.16-35' or a specific CIDR (ex: 198.5.47.-).
                    FromPort: "22" # Replace with the port you want publicly accessible via SSH from AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example '30' or any specific outbound connection like (from other ports) that require private key authentication.
                    ToPort: "22" # Replace with the port you want publicly accessible via SSH from AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example '30' or any specific outbound connection like (from other ports) that require private key authentication.
                - 
                    CidrIp: "1" # Replace with actual cidr value which are accessible only via HTTPS from AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example IP ranges like '0.0.-34' or a specific range (ex: 5678).
                    FromPort: "443" # Replace with the port you want publicly accessible via HTTPS from AWS EC2 instance in same VPC and region as this SecurityGroup will be attached to, for example '90'.
                    ToPort: "443"  # replace with the outbound connection like (to other ports) which require SSL/HTTPS protocol access. In case of IPs range set it on port only(like cidr ranges). Example value is either same as From_port or less than from-ports
